what_it_does: Renders an HTML comparison page that shows a source slide image beside generated artifactCluster examples in dense, sparse, and hybrid real-image configurations.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts and shared CSS variables from /surface/styles/vars.css.
Uses inline CSS for page layout, typography, frames, captions, and responsive wrapping.
Displays the source image /review/ingest/deck1-2026/p-05.jpg with explanatory captions.
Creates three generated comparison panels by setting innerHTML from DNA.org.artifactCluster calls.
The hybrid example passes /review/ingest/deck1-2026/p-08.jpg as the real asset.
standouts: The page is explicitly framed as an acceptance gate between a source slide and synthetic artefact clusters.
It compares multiple configurations of the same organism: dense, sparse, and dense with a real image.
The explanatory copy states that the synthetic cluster is intended to stand in for a scattered artefact pile without using real images.
conventions: Uses a single self-contained HTML document with inline styling and script initialization at the end of the body.
Follows a side-by-side comparison layout using repeated .cmp and .col structures.
Uses local absolute asset paths and runtime paths rooted at /.
Uses innerHTML injection for generated organism markup.
concerns: The acceptance gate is presented visually but no explicit pass/fail criteria are encoded in the file.
The remote font dependency can affect rendering if the font request fails.
The local absolute paths require a specific server root layout.
The inline margin style on the second comparison row breaks the otherwise class-based styling pattern.
notes: This file is a visual review harness for comparing a source slide’s artefact pile concept against generated artifactCluster variants.
questions: What concrete acceptance criteria should be used when viewing this gate?
Are the referenced local assets and runtime scripts guaranteed to exist at these absolute paths?
Should the hybrid panel intentionally use p-08 while the source comparison references p-05?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it presents a source slide and generated artifactCluster variants for visual comparison.
intention_signals: It seems intended to validate whether a generated artifactCluster organism can visually communicate the scattered artefact pile shown in the referenced source slide, including a dense synthetic state and a hybrid real-asset state.
novelty: A compact visual gate combines source imagery, synthetic organism output, and hybrid organism output in one local review page.
contribution: Contributes a file-local comparison harness that keeps the source reference, generated dense and sparse artifact cluster variants, and a real-asset hybrid example together for visual review in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: HTML review page comparing a source deck slide image with dense, sparse, and hybrid DNA.org.artifactCluster renderings using local runtime scripts and inline styling.
intention_for_embedding: Built to check whether synthetic artifactCluster output can stand in for the source slide’s scattered artefact pile concept without relying on real images except in the hybrid example.
